items.prompt: Which sentence best summarizes the first three paragraphs of the text?

options.text: Unlike most other days, Nyota gets to travel far from her home with her father.
For the first time, Nyota is going with her father to observe the gorillas that he has been hired to protect.
Nyota is going to where her father works for the government protecting gorillas.
Nyota wakes up prepared to walk with her father into the green mountains to see what he does for a job.

items.prompt: Which sentence from the text best supports the inference that a knowledgeable guide is needed to find gorillas in the forest?

options.text: “ ‘The females might be half that size, but they would still weigh twice as much as you.’ ”
“Mutimura cleared a path for walking.”
“Now it was late morning, and the damp air was full of birds singing and insects buzzing.”
“ ‘This,’ he said, pointing to an evergreen tree with bent and broken branches dangling close to the ground.”

items.prompt: What is the most likely reason the author included the details about Nyota seeing monkeys around her house in the text?

options.text: to contrast the experience of Nyota watching the mountain gorillas
to emphasize why Mutimura was hired by the government in that area
to provide background information about the life of Nyota and Mutimura
to explain how Nyota became interested in seeing gorillas with Mutimura

items.prompt:
Read the sentence from the text.
Ferns and vines merged together, almost concealing the sky.
What is an antonym, or opposite, of the word concealing as it is used in the sentence?

options.text: exposing
protecting
reflecting
transporting

items.prompt: What inference can be made about Mutimura’s relationship with the gorillas? Support your answer with details from the text.
Click on the statement that best provides an inference that can be made about the narrator’s feelings toward gorillas.

options.text: She cares what happens to the gorillas’ home.
She worries whether the gorillas are getting enough rest.
She wishes the younger gorillas were treated better by their elders.
She is surprised by the relationship the young gorillas have with their parents.

items.prompt: How does the author develop the relationship between the silverback gorilla and the other gorillas? Select three options.

options.text: by narrating how the silverback acts with a young gorilla
by narrating how a silverback provides food for his troops
by having Mutimura explain that the silverback is a troop’s leader
by having Nyota express curiosity about how the silverback treated another gorilla
by having Nyota ask Mutimura a question about the silverback’s role in the troop
by narrating how another gorilla acts toward the silverback

items.prompt: Summarize what happens after Nyota first sees the gorillas. Use key events from the text to support your summary.
